were a professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") team based in [San Diego](/wiki/San_Diego "San Diego"), California, that competed in the [International Hockey League](/wiki/International_Hockey_League_(1945%E2%80%932001) "International Hockey League (1945–2001)") (IHL). The team, the second to use the *Gulls* nickname, was founded in 1990. The team played its home games at the [San Diego Sports Arena](/wiki/San_Diego_Sports_Arena "San Diego Sports Arena").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [San Diego Gulls (1990–95)](/w/index.php?title=San_Diego_Gulls_(1990%E2%80%9395)&redirect=no "San Diego Gulls (1990–95)"))

Professional ice hockey team

For other uses, see [San Diego Gulls (disambiguation)](/wiki/San_Diego_Gulls_(disambiguation) "San Diego Gulls (disambiguation)").

| San Diego Gulls | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| City | [San Diego, California](/wiki/San_Diego "San Diego") |
| League | [International Hockey League](/wiki/International_Hockey_League_(1945%E2%80%932001) "International Hockey League (1945–2001)") |
| Division | Southwest |
| Founded | 1990 |
| Operated | 1990-1995 |
| Folded | 2007 |
| Home arena | [San Diego Sports Arena](/wiki/San_Diego_Sports_Arena "San Diego Sports Arena") |
| Colors | Red, black |
| Franchise history | |
| 1990–1995 | San Diego Gulls |
| 1995–1996 | [Los Angeles Ice Dogs](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Ice_Dogs "Los Angeles Ice Dogs") |
| 1996–2007 | [Long Beach Ice Dogs](/wiki/Long_Beach_Ice_Dogs "Long Beach Ice Dogs") |

The **San Diego Gulls** were a professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") team based in [San Diego](/wiki/San_Diego "San Diego"), California, that competed in the [International Hockey League](/wiki/International_Hockey_League_(1945%E2%80%932001) "International Hockey League (1945–2001)") (IHL). The team, the second to use the *Gulls* nickname, was founded in 1990. The team played its home games at the [San Diego Sports Arena](/wiki/San_Diego_Sports_Arena "San Diego Sports Arena").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=San_Diego_Gulls_(1990%E2%80%931995)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The team played five seasons, then relocated to [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"), in 1995 to become the [Los Angeles Ice Dogs](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Ice_Dogs "Los Angeles Ice Dogs"). After one season in Los Angeles, the team moved to [Long Beach](/wiki/Long_Beach,_California "Long Beach, California") to become the [Long Beach Ice Dogs](/wiki/Long_Beach_Ice_Dogs "Long Beach Ice Dogs") and later would leave the IHL and join the WCHL (West Coast Hockey League).

The Gulls were coached by [Mike O'Connell](/wiki/Mike_O%27Connell "Mike O'Connell") (1990–91), [Don Waddell](/wiki/Don_Waddell "Don Waddell") (1991–92), [Rick Dudley](/wiki/Rick_Dudley "Rick Dudley") (1992–93), [Harold Snepsts](/wiki/Harold_Snepsts "Harold Snepsts") (1993–94), and [Walt Kyle](/wiki/Walt_Kyle "Walt Kyle") (1994–95). In the 1992–93 season, the Gulls won the [Fred A. Huber Trophy](/wiki/Fred_A._Huber_Trophy "Fred A. Huber Trophy"), finishing first place overall in the regular season, setting an IHL record of 132 points earned as a team. The Gulls reached the [Turner Cup](/wiki/Turner_Cup "Turner Cup") finals, led by goaltender [Clint Malarchuk](/wiki/Clint_Malarchuk "Clint Malarchuk"), but were swept in four games by the [Fort Wayne Komets](/wiki/Fort_Wayne_Komets "Fort Wayne Komets").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

*Legend*: **OL**=Overtime loss, **Pct**=Winning percentage[[3]](#cite_note-3)

| Season | Games | Won | Lost | OL | Points | Pct % | Goals for | Goals against | Playoffs |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1990–91 | 83 | 30 | 45 | 8 | 68 | 0.410 | 273 | 362 | Out of Playoffs |
| 1991–92 | 82 | 45 | 28 | 9 | 99 | 0.604 | 340 | 298 | Lost in round 1 |
| 1992–93 | 82 | 62 | 12 | 8 | 132 | 0.805 | 381 | 229 | Lost in finals |
| 1993–94 | 81 | 42 | 28 | 11 | 95 | 0.586 | 311 | 302 | Lost in round 2 |
| 1994–95 | 81 | 37 | 36 | 8 | 82 | 0.506 | 268 | 301 | Lost in round 1 |
